rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WILLIAMS VS. SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA TEH NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Orders Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted And Orders Granting Motions For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 10, PLAINTIFF KENNETH WILLIAMS' Motion For Relief From Orders Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted And Orders Granting Motions For Judgment On The Pleadings. Plaintiff Kenneth Williams' motion for relief from orders deeming requests for admissions admitted and orders granting motions for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The sole grounds for this motion is the asserted misconduct of the A1 Law Group constitutes extrinsic fraud. However, the record shows that plaintiff was aware of and had an opportunity to oppose the motions which he seeks to have set aside and thus any fraud perpetrated against him is intrinsic fraud which is not a basis to set aside otherwise valid court orders. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 5, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BREX INC VS. DARKSTORE, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ron Chow; And [Proposed] Orde
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 1, PLAINTIFF BREX INC'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Plaintiff Brex Inc.'s motion to set aside the dismissal dated November 18, 2025 is granted. Brex has shown that the dismissal was the result of the mistake, inadvertence and/or excusable neglect of its counsel. No opposition filed. The dismissal is vacated and the case is restored to the civil active list. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. rulings.Case Title: A2 ACADEMY LLC ET AL VS. SWORD HEALTH, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 5, Defendants' motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted in part. If they have not already done so, no later than March 10, 2026 plaintiffs must produce all responsive documents for the five time periods which they now contend that defendants made equity offers. No later than April 30, 2026 plaintiffs must identify any further equity offers which they intend to seek relief at trial and produce all responsive documents for any of those equity offers which they had not previously produced all responsive documents. Plaintiffs may not seek relief at trial as to any equity offers that they have not produced all responsive documents by April 30, 2026. rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants Atlassian Usa Inc Notice Of Taking Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 11, PLAINTIFF SARKIS GEGAMYAN'S Motion To Quash Defendants Atlassian Usa Inc Notice Of Taking Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan's motion to quash the notice of taking his continued deposition is denied. Plaintiff failed to meet and confer prior to filing this motion. Even more significantly, page 277 of the transcript of the first session of his deposition shows that his then attorney agreed to additional "days" of his deposition. Nor does plaintiff provide any support for any cognizable reason to avoid a further session of his deposition. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LINDA HARRINGTON ET AL VS. ALEJANDRA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 8, DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record Defendant Alejandra Garcia's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. No later than March 5, 2026 plaintiff Linda Harrington must serve verified code-complaint responses to the form interrogatories served on her and the only objections she may assert are privilege and work product. No later than March 19, 2026 plaintiff must pay $1,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Garcia. In the exercise of its discretion, the court will not entertain live evidence on this motion. Even if plaintiff was not able to respond to the interrogatories on a timely basis, it was her obligation to notify Garcia on a timely basis that she was unable to do so, which she did not. If the responses are served by March 5, it would be inequitable to preclude plaintiff from asserting any privilege and work product objections she may have, but nor is it equitable to allow plaintiff to assert other objections. For a simple motion, Garcia's request for $3,100 in monetary sanctions is excessive. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LESLEY GISELLE HOWARD VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Employment Law
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Employment Law Defendant City and County of San Francisco' motion for sanction for failure to comply with discovery order is granted in part. Plaintiff is precluded from introducing any evidence or making any argument at trial as to any subject that is covered by employment law form interrogatories 201.3, 203.1, 207.2 and 213.2 that she did not provide a response enabling the City to understand and defend against. This issue sanction sufficiently protects the City from being surprised at trial and accepts the representation by plaintiff that she has fully responded to the interrogatories. Monetary sanctions are denied because the plaintiff did not act without substantial justification in opposing this motion. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI CINCOTTA VS. ALEXANDER KALLIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 2, 2026, line 9, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER KALLIS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Alexander Kallis' anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sole cause of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Giovanni Cincotta is granted. Kallis has shown that the sole cause arises from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute and Cincotta has not submitted any evidence showing that his cause of action has minimal merit. No opposition filed. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026.
